summary: Orchestrator writes .current-run-status.json for live dashboard monitoring
context: Dashboard needs to show live run progress. Cloud and local dashboard both need a way to poll current status.
rationale: A simple JSON file in the results directory is the lowest-friction approach. The orchestrator writes it on every progress event (best-effort, non-blocking), and deletes it when the run completes. The dashboard GET /api/status reads this file. No WebSocket or SSE infrastructure needed.

alternatives.option: WebSocket connection from dashboard to orchestrator
alternatives.reason_rejected: Requires keeping a WebSocket server running alongside the orchestrator, adding complexity for a polling-adequate use case

alternatives.option: Write status to a database or shared memory
alternatives.reason_rejected: Over-engineered for a single-writer single-reader status check
